Fact: Magmar and Electabuzz seem to be counterparts; both evolve in a very similar way (first at level 30, then after being traded holding an item which has a name that ends with the suffix "-izer"), both have the same gender ratio (75% male, 25% female), and both are often exclusive to one version of a pair of games (Magmar is exclusive to Pokémon Blue, LeafGreen, Black 2, and Shining Pearl, while Electabuzz is exclusive to Pokémon Red, FireRed, White 2, and Brilliant Diamond). They both have similar status-inducing Abilities (Flame Body and Static), as well as the same Hidden Ability, Vital Spirit. Since Generation V, they also often learn similar moves at the same level. They even have the same special moves when purified in Pokémon XD: Follow Me and Cross Chop. Their evolutionary relatives were released in the same generation (Generation II and IV). Both also appear to be based on beings from Japanese mythology; Magmar has similarities to the Karura and Electabuzz has similarities to the oni.
Magmar and Electabuzz are the only version-exclusive pair from Pokémon Red and Blue to not be version-exclusive in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!.
Magmar was featured in the tenth episode of the Beyond the Pokédex series.
Magmar is one of four Pokémon to be capable of having every Ribbon that can be obtained without events, the others being Magmortar, Magneton, and Magnezone.
